question: What is the minimum viable improvement rate? Quantify by session type (DOMEX vs non-DOMEX), avg_lp fragility, and minimum DOMEX%.
expect: avg_lp=2.0 is fragile (margin near zero); DOMEX sessions have higher merge_rate than non-DOMEX; minimum DOMEX% for merge_rate >= 72% is ~35%
method: Parsed SWARM-LANES.md (current file + lane_history.py git-log), SESSION-LOG.md, 8 historical eval-sufficiency artifacts (S193-S413), eval_sufficiency.py live run at S413

domex_domain_specific.note: All domain-specific DOMEX lanes (META, NK, EVAL, EXP, etc.)

merge_rate_by_session_type.key_finding: DOMEX vs non-DOMEX merge rate difference is -2.0pp (90.6% vs 92.6%). NON-DOMEX lanes merge at HIGHER rate. DOMEX% does NOT predict merge_rate directionally. Both types benefit equally from close_lane.py structural enforcement.

historical_failure_s309.cause: 140 stale OPEN/ACTIVE lanes inflating denominator (eval_sufficiency formula), not low DOMEX%
historical_failure_s309.lesson: High lesson rate cannot compensate for collapsed merge_rate

recent_20_sessions.note: EXTREMELY low n. Only 2 sessions in a 20-session window because S402-S412 not yet logged.

avg_lp_fragility.explanation: avg_lp=2.0 is a measurement artifact of n=2. The 20-session window (S381-S401) contains only 2 sessions with SESSION-LOG entries because S402-S412 have not been logged yet. When those sessions get logged, avg_lp will almost certainly drop below 2.0 unless each averages exactly 2.0 LP. The 50-session window (n=19) shows avg_lp=1.842, which is the more robust estimate. All-time avg_lp=1.92 is also below 2.0.

minimum_domex_pct.question: What minimum DOMEX% is needed to sustain merge_rate >= 72%?
minimum_domex_pct.answer: DOMEX% is NOT the relevant variable. Both DOMEX (90.6%) and non-DOMEX (92.6%) lanes have nearly identical merge rates. The S309 failure (14.6%) was caused by stale OPEN/ACTIVE lane accumulation, not by lane type.

analysis.direction: Non-DOMEX lanes merge at HIGHER rate (counter to hypothesis)
analysis.real_driver: close_lane.py enforcement prevents stale lane accumulation. Both types benefit equally.
analysis.s309_root_cause: 140 stale OPEN/ACTIVE lanes in denominator (eval_sufficiency formula: MERGED/(MERGED+OPEN+ACTIVE))

theoretical_minimum_domex_pct.note: Worst case assumes non-DOMEX rate degrades to 14.6% (S309 level). Current data shows non-DOMEX=92.6%, making DOMEX% irrelevant.

minimum_domex_pct.practical_answer: Any DOMEX% works as long as close_lane.py enforcement is active and stale lanes are triaged. The minimum DOMEX% question is misframed.

composite_sensitivity.key_insight: Proxy-K is the cheapest improvement: fixing drift<6% raises P from 1 to 2 (composite 2.0->2.25). This compensates for avg_lp dropping to 1.5 (I goes 2->1 but P goes 1->2, net zero).

findings: F1: avg_lp=2.0 is a MEASUREMENT ARTIFACT based on n=2 sessions. The robust 50-session estimate is 1.842 (below threshold). All-time average is 1.92. The 2.0 threshold is NOT sustainably met.

F2: DOMEX% does NOT predict merge_rate. Non-DOMEX lanes (92.6%) merge at a HIGHER rate than DOMEX (90.6%). Both types benefit from close_lane.py enforcement equally. The minimum DOMEX% question is misframed.

F3: The S309 failure (composite 0.333) was caused by stale OPEN/ACTIVE lane accumulation (140 lanes in denominator), not by lane type or lesson rate. avg_lp was 3.0 at the time, proving lesson rate cannot compensate for collapsed merge_rate.

F4: The binding constraint for composite improvement is proxy-K drift (Protect score), not avg_lp. Fixing proxy-K from 7.4% to <6% raises composite from 2.0 to 2.25 and provides a buffer against avg_lp decline.

F5: LP distribution is bimodal: 41.9% of sessions produce 0 LP, 34.6% produce >= 2 LP. Median LP per session is 1. The avg_lp=2.0 threshold requires sustained high-output sessions to offset the majority of 0-LP sessions.

F6: The eval_sufficiency recent-20 window will become volatile as S402-S412 get logged. If those sessions average 1 LP each, avg_lp drops to ~1.2, causing Increase to fall from 2 to 1 and composite from 2.0 to 1.75 (PARTIAL).
actual: avg_lp=2.0 is a measurement artifact (n=2), not a stable achievement. Robust estimate is 1.842. DOMEX% is irrelevant to merge_rate. S309 failure was stale-lane accumulation, not session type. Binding constraint is proxy-K drift, not lesson rate.
diff: Expect was partially wrong: (1) DOMEX has LOWER merge rate than non-DOMEX (reversed direction), (2) margin is not 'near zero' but exactly zero (worse than expected), (3) minimum DOMEX% question is misframed because DOMEX% does not predict merge_rate.
prescriptions: P1: Log S402-S412 sessions to SESSION-LOG.md immediately — current avg_lp=2.0 is based on n=2 and will change.
P2: Run compact.py to reduce proxy-K drift below 6% — cheapest composite improvement (1→2 for Protect).
P3: Monitor stale OPEN/ACTIVE lane count, not DOMEX%. Lane staleness is the merge_rate driver, not session type.
P4: Consider widening eval_sufficiency window from 20 to 50 sessions for avg_lp to reduce sensitivity to log gaps.
